controls.name: Data Classification Policy
controls.description: Establish and maintain a formal data classification policy that defines classification levels (e.g., Public, Internal, Confidential, Restricted), handling requirements for each level, and roles responsible for classification decisions.

legal: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.12 — Classification of information
BNM RMiT S10.4 — data classification based on sensitivity
technical: Automated classification tools integrated with DLP
Metadata tagging on all structured and unstructured data stores
governance: Annual review of classification policy by CISO
Classification training for all data owners

keyActivities.activity: Define classification taxonomy (minimum 4 levels)
Map regulatory requirements to classification levels
Train data owners on classification procedures
Review and update classification decisions

basic.description: Manual classification with basic labeling. Classification policy exists but enforcement is inconsistent.

mature.description: Automated classification tools deployed. Regular audits of classification accuracy. Integration with DLP policies.

advanced.description: AI-driven classification with continuous learning. Real-time classification at data creation. Full metadata lineage tracking.

controls.name: Data Labeling & Marking
controls.description: Implement consistent labeling and marking of data assets according to their classification level, including visual headers/footers for documents, metadata tags for electronic files, and physical markings for printed materials.

legal: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.13 — Labelling of information
NIST 800-171 3.13.2 — CUI marking
technical: Automated document header/footer injection
Email subject-line classification tags
Database column-level sensitivity labels

keyActivities.activity: Define visual labeling standards for each classification level
Deploy automated labeling tools (Microsoft AIP, Boldon James, etc.)
Audit labeling accuracy on sample datasets
Update labeling rules for new data types

basic.description: Manual labeling with visual markers. Inconsistent application across departments.

mature.description: Automated labeling integrated with classification engine. Consistent metadata tagging across all major data stores.

advanced.description: Content-aware labeling with NLP-based classification. Persistent labels that travel with data across systems and cloud services.

controls.name: Data Handling Procedures
controls.description: Define and enforce data handling procedures for each classification level covering creation, storage, transmission, sharing, archival, and destruction of data assets.

legal: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.10 — Acceptable use of information assets
BNM RMiT S10.5 — data handling controls
technical: DLP policies aligned to handling rules
Access controls enforcing need-to-know for classified data
governance: Handling procedures documented per classification level

keyActivities.activity: Document handling procedures for each classification level
Implement technical controls enforcing handling rules
Conduct data handling awareness training
Audit compliance with handling procedures

basic.description: Written handling procedures exist. Manual enforcement relies on user awareness.

mature.description: Technical controls enforce most handling rules. DLP monitors for violations. Regular compliance audits.

advanced.description: Automated policy enforcement across all channels. Real-time violation alerts with automated remediation workflows.

controls.name: Data Inventory & Mapping
controls.description: Maintain a comprehensive inventory of data assets including their locations, owners, classification levels, processing activities, and data flows across systems and third parties.

legal: PCI DSS 3.2.1 — Account for stored cardholder data
ISO 27001:2022 A.5.9 — Inventory of information assets
technical: Automated data discovery and scanning tools
Data flow mapping across internal and external systems
governance: Data inventory reviewed and updated quarterly
Data owners assigned for all classified assets

keyActivities.activity: Deploy automated data discovery tools
Map data flows across systems and third parties
Assign and validate data ownership
Reconcile data inventory with asset management

basic.description: Spreadsheet-based inventory of major data stores. Manual updates by data owners.

mature.description: Automated discovery tools maintain near-real-time inventory. Data flow diagrams current and version-controlled.

advanced.description: AI-driven data cataloging with automatic classification. Real-time data lineage tracking across multi-cloud environments.

controls.name: Metadata Management & Tagging
controls.description: Implement consistent metadata standards and tagging practices to support classification, discovery, retention, and governance of data assets across structured and unstructured data stores.

legal: ISO 27001:2022 A.5.12 — Classification of information (metadata requirements)
technical: Standardized metadata schema across data platforms
Automated metadata extraction and enrichment
governance: Metadata quality metrics tracked and reported

keyActivities.activity: Define organization-wide metadata taxonomy
Implement metadata tagging automation
Monitor metadata quality and completeness
Align metadata schema with regulatory requirements

basic.description: Basic metadata fields defined. Manual tagging for key data assets.

mature.description: Automated metadata extraction. Enterprise data catalog with search and discovery capabilities.

advanced.description: ML-driven metadata enrichment. Knowledge graph linking data assets, owners, regulations, and processing activities.

controls.name: Full-Disk Encryption
controls.description: Implement full-disk encryption (FDE) on all endpoints, removable media, and servers storing classified data using approved cryptographic algorithms (AES-256 or equivalent).

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Render PAN unreadable
BNM RMiT S10.46 — encryption for confidential data at rest
technical: AES-256 or equivalent algorithm
TPM-based key storage for endpoints
Central management console for encryption status
governance: Encryption policy mandating FDE for all endpoints

keyActivities.activity: Deploy FDE to all endpoints (BitLocker, FileVault, LUKS)
Monitor encryption compliance dashboard
Manage recovery keys in escrow
Test recovery procedures quarterly

basic.description: FDE enabled on company-issued laptops. Manual compliance tracking via spreadsheet.

mature.description: Centrally managed FDE across all endpoints. Automated compliance reporting. Key escrow with documented recovery procedures.

advanced.description: Hardware-based encryption with TPM 2.0. Self-encrypting drives (SEDs) for servers. Automated remediation of non-compliant devices.

controls.name: Database Encryption (TDE)
controls.description: Implement Transparent Data Encryption (TDE) for databases storing sensitive data, ensuring data files, log files, and backup files are encrypted without application changes.

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Render stored PAN unreadable
BNM RMiT S10.46 — encryption for databases
technical: TDE enabled on all production databases with sensitive data
Database encryption key (DEK) protected by master key in HSM
Encrypted backups verified through restore testing
governance: Quarterly audit of TDE status across all database instances

keyActivities.activity: Enable TDE on all databases with classified data
Store database master keys in HSM
Verify encrypted backup restorability
Audit TDE compliance across database estate

basic.description: TDE enabled on primary production databases. Manual key management.

mature.description: TDE across all database instances. HSM-backed key management. Automated compliance reporting.

advanced.description: Column-level encryption for highly sensitive fields. Key rotation automated with zero-downtime. Encrypted query processing capabilities.

controls.name: File-Level Encryption
controls.description: Implement file-level encryption for sensitive documents and files stored on file shares, cloud storage, and collaboration platforms using rights-managed encryption.

legal: NIST 800-171 3.13.11 — CUI encryption at rest
ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 — Use of cryptography
technical: AES-256 encryption for files at rest
Integration with document management systems
Rights management preventing unauthorized decryption
governance: File encryption standards for each classification level
User training on file encryption tools

keyActivities.activity: Deploy file encryption solution (Azure RMS, Vera, etc.)
Define encryption rules by file type and classification
Train users on file encryption procedures
Audit file encryption compliance on shared storage

basic.description: Manual file encryption using native OS tools. Users responsible for encrypting sensitive files.

mature.description: Automated file encryption based on classification labels. Rights management controls access after encryption.

advanced.description: Persistent encryption that follows files across platforms. Integration with DRM for granular access control.

controls.name: Cloud Storage Encryption
controls.description: Ensure all data stored in cloud services (IaaS, PaaS, SaaS) is encrypted at rest using customer-managed keys (CMK) or cloud-provider managed keys with appropriate key management controls.

legal: CSA CCM CEK-04 — cloud encryption key management
BNM RMiT S10.47 — encryption for cloud-hosted data
technical: Server-side encryption (SSE) enabled for all cloud storage
Customer-managed keys (CMK) for sensitive data
Key management separate from data storage
governance: Cloud provider encryption capabilities assessed during procurement

keyActivities.activity: Enable server-side encryption on all cloud storage
Implement CMK for sensitive workloads
Monitor cloud encryption compliance
Assess cloud provider key management practices

basic.description: Cloud-provider default encryption enabled. Provider-managed keys used.

mature.description: Customer-managed keys (CMK) for all sensitive data. Centralized cloud key management. Automated compliance monitoring.

advanced.description: BYOK/HYOK across all cloud providers. Client-side encryption before upload. Multi-cloud key management with unified policy.

controls.name: Key Escrow & Recovery
controls.description: Maintain secure key escrow procedures ensuring encrypted data remains recoverable in case of key loss, employee departure, or system failure while preventing unauthorized access.

legal: NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 — key recovery recommendations
technical: Encrypted key escrow database with multi-party access
Hardware-backed key escrow (HSM)
Automated key recovery workflows
governance: Key escrow policy with dual-control access
Regular testing of key recovery procedures

keyActivities.activity: Implement key escrow with dual-control access
Test key recovery procedures
Audit escrow access logs
Update escrow procedures for departing employees

basic.description: Basic key escrow using encrypted file storage. Manual recovery process documented.

mature.description: HSM-backed key escrow with dual-control access. Automated workflows for common recovery scenarios.

advanced.description: M-of-N key splitting for critical keys. Automated escrow lifecycle management with audit trails.

controls.name: TLS Configuration & Enforcement
controls.description: Enforce TLS 1.2 or higher for all data in transit, including web applications, APIs, internal services, and database connections. Disable deprecated protocols (SSL, TLS 1.0, TLS 1.1).

legal: PCI DSS 4.2.1 — Strong cryptography for PAN transmission
BNM RMiT S10.48 — encryption for data in transit
technical: TLS 1.2+ enforced on all endpoints
Strong cipher suites only (AES-GCM, ChaCha20-Poly1305)
HSTS headers on all web applications

keyActivities.activity: Enforce TLS 1.2+ across all services
Disable deprecated TLS/SSL versions
Scan for weak cipher suites (SSL Labs, testssl.sh)
Implement HSTS on all web applications

basic.description: TLS 1.2 enabled on external-facing services. Some internal services may use older protocols.

mature.description: TLS 1.2+ enforced organization-wide. Automated scanning for weak configurations. HSTS deployed.

advanced.description: TLS 1.3 preferred everywhere. Certificate Transparency monitoring. Automated cipher suite rotation.

controls.name: Certificate Management
controls.description: Maintain a comprehensive certificate lifecycle management program covering issuance, renewal, revocation, and monitoring of X.509 certificates used for TLS, code signing, and authentication.

legal: PCI DSS 4.2.1 — trusted certificates for transmission security
technical: Automated certificate lifecycle management (ACME)
Certificate transparency log monitoring
Internal PKI or trusted CA partnership

keyActivities.activity: Maintain complete certificate inventory
Implement automated certificate renewal (ACME/Let's Encrypt)
Monitor for certificate expiry (30/14/7-day alerts)
Review and rotate intermediate CA certificates

basic.description: Spreadsheet-based certificate tracking. Manual renewal processes.

mature.description: Automated certificate management platform. Expiry alerting with escalation. Internal PKI for internal services.

advanced.description: Fully automated certificate lifecycle. Short-lived certificates (90 days). Post-quantum cryptography readiness assessment.

controls.name: VPN & Tunnel Encryption
controls.description: Implement encrypted VPN tunnels for remote access and site-to-site connectivity using IPsec or WireGuard with approved cryptographic algorithms.

legal: NIST 800-171 3.13.8 — Cryptographic protection for CUI in transit
technical: IPsec or WireGuard with AES-256-GCM
Perfect forward secrecy (PFS) enabled
Split tunneling disabled for classified traffic
governance: VPN access logs retained and reviewed

keyActivities.activity: Deploy VPN with approved encryption standards
Enforce PFS on all VPN connections
Review VPN access logs for anomalies
Test VPN failover and redundancy

basic.description: VPN deployed for remote access with basic encryption. Manual log review.

mature.description: Site-to-site and remote VPN with PFS. Centralized VPN management. Automated anomaly detection on VPN logs.

advanced.description: Zero-trust network access (ZTNA) complementing VPN. Adaptive authentication based on risk scoring.

controls.name: API Encryption & Security
controls.description: Ensure all API communications use TLS encryption with mutual authentication (mTLS) for service-to-service calls and proper token management for API authentication.

legal: PCI DSS 4.2.1 — Strong cryptography for data in transit
BNM RMiT S10.48 — API security
technical: mTLS for service-to-service communication
OAuth 2.0 / OIDC for API authentication
API gateway enforcing encryption policies
governance: API inventory maintained with encryption status

keyActivities.activity: Enforce TLS on all API endpoints
Implement mTLS for internal service mesh
Deploy API gateway with encryption enforcement
Audit API encryption compliance

basic.description: TLS on external APIs. Basic API key authentication.

mature.description: mTLS for internal APIs. OAuth 2.0 with short-lived tokens. API gateway with policy enforcement.

advanced.description: Service mesh with automatic mTLS. API-level encryption for sensitive payloads. Zero-trust API architecture.

controls.name: Email Encryption
controls.description: Implement email encryption for sensitive communications using S/MIME, PGP, or transport-level encryption (STARTTLS, MTA-STS) to protect email content in transit.

legal: PCI DSS 4.2.1 — Encryption for PAN in transit
ISO 27001:2022 A.8.24 — Cryptography for email
technical: S/MIME or PGP for end-to-end email encryption
STARTTLS enforced on mail servers
MTA-STS and DANE for mail transport security
governance: Email encryption policy for classified communications
User training on secure email practices

keyActivities.activity: Enable STARTTLS on all mail servers
Deploy S/MIME certificates for users handling sensitive data
Implement MTA-STS for mail transport security
Train users on email encryption usage

basic.description: STARTTLS enabled on mail servers. Manual PGP/S/MIME for specific users.

mature.description: Automated email encryption based on classification labels. Gateway-based encryption for external recipients.

advanced.description: End-to-end encryption with automated key exchange. DLP-triggered email encryption. MTA-STS with DANE.

controls.name: Confidential Computing
controls.description: Implement confidential computing technologies that protect data during processing using hardware-based Trusted Execution Environments (TEEs) such as Intel SGX, AMD SEV, or ARM TrustZone.

legal: Emerging regulatory requirements for data-in-use protection
technical: TEE-enabled hardware (Intel SGX, AMD SEV, ARM TrustZone)
Attestation services for TEE integrity verification
Secure enclave programming frameworks
governance: Confidential computing use cases identified and prioritized

keyActivities.activity: Assess data-in-use protection requirements
Pilot confidential computing for high-value workloads
Implement TEE attestation and verification
Monitor confidential computing technology maturity

basic.description: Awareness of confidential computing. Use case identification completed.

mature.description: TEE deployed for high-sensitivity workloads. Attestation services operational.

advanced.description: Multi-party confidential computing for cross-organization data processing. Full attestation chain with hardware root of trust.

controls.name: Secure Enclaves
controls.description: Deploy hardware-isolated secure enclaves for processing highly sensitive data, ensuring that even privileged system administrators and hypervisors cannot access data within the enclave.

legal: Regulatory drivers for processing sensitive data securely (e.g., financial data, PII)
technical: Intel SGX or AMD SEV-SNP enabled workloads
Enclave attestation and sealing
Secure key provisioning to enclaves

keyActivities.activity: Define workloads requiring enclave protection
Deploy enclave-enabled infrastructure
Implement remote attestation for enclaves
Test side-channel attack resilience

basic.description: Secure enclave feasibility assessed. Pilot workloads identified.

mature.description: Production enclaves for sensitive processing. Remote attestation operational.

advanced.description: Multi-enclave architectures with secure communication. Side-channel hardened configurations.

controls.name: Homomorphic Encryption
controls.description: Evaluate and implement homomorphic encryption (HE) schemes for use cases requiring computation on encrypted data without decryption, such as privacy-preserving analytics.

legal: Privacy regulations requiring minimization of data exposure
technical: HE library selection (SEAL, HElib, TFHE)
Performance benchmarking for target use cases
Integration with existing data pipelines
governance: HE use case assessment and feasibility study

keyActivities.activity: Identify use cases suitable for homomorphic encryption
Benchmark HE performance for candidate workloads
Pilot HE implementation for selected use case
Track HE technology maturity and standards

basic.description: HE feasibility assessed. No production implementation.

mature.description: HE deployed for specific privacy-preserving analytics use cases.

advanced.description: Practical FHE (fully homomorphic encryption) for multi-party computation scenarios.

controls.name: Tokenization for In-Use Protection
controls.description: Implement tokenization to replace sensitive data elements with non-sensitive tokens during processing, reducing exposure of actual data values in application memory and processing pipelines.

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Tokenization as alternative to encryption for PAN
Regulatory requirements to minimize data exposure
technical: Tokenization vault with high availability
Format-preserving tokenization (FPE) where needed
Detokenization restricted to authorized processes
governance: Token vault access controls reviewed quarterly

keyActivities.activity: Identify data elements for tokenization
Deploy tokenization platform
Restrict detokenization access to authorized services
Audit tokenization coverage and access

basic.description: Tokenization for PCI scope reduction. Single tokenization vault.

mature.description: Enterprise tokenization platform. Multi-data-type tokenization. Automated detokenization policies.

advanced.description: Distributed tokenization with micro-vaults. Format-preserving encryption/tokenization across all sensitive data types.

controls.name: Network DLP
controls.description: Deploy network-based Data Loss Prevention sensors at key egress points to inspect, detect, and block unauthorized transmission of sensitive data over network channels.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.55 — Data leakage prevention
PCI DSS 12.5.2 — Prevent data exfiltration
technical: Inline or passive DLP sensors at internet egress
Content inspection for sensitive data patterns (PAN, PII, classified content)
Integration with email gateway and web proxy
governance: DLP policy aligned to data classification

keyActivities.activity: Deploy network DLP at all internet egress points
Create DLP policies for each data classification level
Tune DLP policies to reduce false positives
Review DLP incident reports

basic.description: Network DLP deployed at primary internet gateway. Basic regex patterns for PAN and SSN detection.

mature.description: Multi-channel DLP (email, web, FTP). Content fingerprinting for classified documents. Tuned policies with low false-positive rates.

advanced.description: ML-based content analysis. Behavioral DLP detecting anomalous data movements. Automated response workflows.

controls.name: Endpoint DLP
controls.description: Deploy endpoint DLP agents on workstations and laptops to monitor and control data transfers via USB, printing, clipboard, screen capture, and local file operations.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.55 — Data leakage prevention at endpoints
technical: Endpoint DLP agent on all managed endpoints
USB/removable media control policies
Print and clipboard monitoring for classified content

keyActivities.activity: Deploy endpoint DLP agent to all managed endpoints
Configure USB and removable media policies
Monitor endpoint DLP alerts
Review endpoint DLP exceptions and overrides

basic.description: Endpoint DLP on high-risk endpoints. USB blocking for general users.

mature.description: Enterprise-wide endpoint DLP. Content-aware USB policies. Clipboard and print monitoring.

advanced.description: Context-aware endpoint DLP with user behavior analytics. Adaptive policies based on risk scoring.

controls.name: Cloud DLP
controls.description: Implement cloud-native DLP controls to discover, classify, and protect sensitive data across SaaS applications, cloud storage, and cloud-hosted databases.

legal: CSA CCM DSP-08 — Cloud data loss prevention
BNM RMiT S10.55 — DLP including cloud channels
technical: CASB or cloud-native DLP for SaaS monitoring
API-based inspection of cloud storage
Cloud DLP integrated with classification engine
governance: Cloud DLP coverage mapped to cloud application inventory
Cloud DLP policy aligned with on-premises DLP

keyActivities.activity: Deploy CASB or cloud-native DLP for sanctioned SaaS
Scan cloud storage for unprotected sensitive data
Integrate cloud DLP with on-premises DLP policies
Monitor cloud DLP alerts and shadow IT detection

basic.description: CASB deployed for major SaaS applications. Basic cloud storage scanning.

mature.description: Unified DLP policy across on-premises and cloud. Real-time cloud DLP with automated remediation.

advanced.description: AI-driven cloud DLP with contextual analysis. Shadow IT discovery and automatic policy enforcement.

controls.name: Email DLP
controls.description: Implement email-specific DLP controls to inspect outbound email content and attachments for sensitive data, enforcing encryption, quarantine, or blocking based on policy.

legal: PCI DSS 4.2.1 — Protect PAN in email
BNM RMiT S10.55 — Email data leakage prevention
technical: Email gateway DLP inspection
Attachment scanning for sensitive content
Automated encryption for emails containing classified data
governance: Email DLP policy aligned with classification

keyActivities.activity: Configure email DLP rules on mail gateway
Implement automatic encryption for sensitive email content
Review email DLP incidents and adjust policies
Train users on secure email practices

basic.description: Basic email DLP rules for PAN and SSN patterns. Manual incident review.

mature.description: Content-aware email DLP with classification label integration. Automated encryption triggers.

advanced.description: ML-based email content analysis. Contextual email DLP with risk-adaptive policies.

controls.name: DLP Policy Tuning & Incident Response
controls.description: Establish ongoing DLP policy tuning processes and incident response procedures to optimize detection accuracy, reduce false positives, and ensure timely response to confirmed data loss incidents.

legal: Regulatory requirements for data breach notification
technical: DLP incident management workflow
False positive tracking and policy refinement
Integration with SIEM for correlated alerting
governance: Monthly DLP metrics reporting to management

keyActivities.activity: Track and analyze DLP false positive rates
Refine DLP detection rules based on incident analysis
Investigate confirmed DLP incidents
Report DLP metrics to management

basic.description: Basic incident tracking. Reactive policy tuning based on complaints.

mature.description: Structured DLP incident workflow. Metrics-driven policy tuning. Integration with SIEM and SOAR.

advanced.description: ML-assisted policy optimization. Automated incident classification and routing. Predictive analytics for data loss risk.

controls.name: Key Generation
controls.description: Establish secure cryptographic key generation procedures using approved random number generators and key lengths appropriate for the protection level required.

legal: NIST SP 800-133 — Key generation recommendations
PCI DSS 3.6.1 — Strong key generation
technical: FIPS 140-2/3 validated RNG or DRBG
Minimum key lengths: AES-256, RSA-2048, ECC P-256
Key generation in HSM or trusted environment
governance: Approved algorithm and key length standards

keyActivities.activity: Define approved cryptographic algorithms and key lengths
Generate cryptographic keys in HSM environment
Validate FIPS compliance of key generation processes
Document key generation events with audit trail

basic.description: Keys generated using operating system RNG. Manual documentation of key generation.

mature.description: HSM-based key generation. FIPS 140-2 Level 3 validated. Automated audit logging.

advanced.description: Hardware RNG entropy sources. Quantum-safe key generation algorithms assessed. Automated key generation workflows.

controls.name: Key Distribution
controls.description: Implement secure key distribution mechanisms ensuring cryptographic keys are transported securely to authorized endpoints and never exposed in plaintext during transit.

legal: NIST SP 800-57 Part 1 — Key distribution guidelines
PCI DSS 3.6.1 — Secure key distribution
technical: Key wrapping using KEK (Key Encryption Key)
Secure key transport protocols (TLS, out-of-band)
Split knowledge and dual control for sensitive keys

keyActivities.activity: Implement secure key distribution channels
Use key wrapping for all key transport operations
Enforce split knowledge for master keys
Audit key distribution logs

basic.description: Manual key distribution with encrypted transport. Basic documentation.

mature.description: Automated key distribution via KMS APIs. Key wrapping with HSM-protected KEK.

advanced.description: Zero-knowledge key distribution protocols. Quantum key distribution (QKD) assessment.

controls.name: Key Storage
controls.description: Implement secure key storage using Hardware Security Modules (HSMs), key vaults, or equivalent tamper-resistant storage ensuring keys are never stored in plaintext.

legal: PCI DSS 3.6.1 — Secure key storage
NIST SP 800-57 — Key storage requirements
technical: HSM (FIPS 140-2/3 Level 3) for master keys
Cloud key vault (AWS KMS, Azure Key Vault, GCP Cloud KMS) for cloud workloads
Keys never stored alongside encrypted data
governance: HSM access restricted to key custodians

keyActivities.activity: Deploy HSMs for master key and KEK storage
Implement cloud key vaults for cloud-hosted keys
Ensure separation of keys and encrypted data
Audit key storage access controls

basic.description: Software-based key storage with access controls. Keys encrypted at rest.

mature.description: HSM-backed key storage for critical keys. Cloud key vaults for cloud workloads. Strict access controls.

advanced.description: FIPS 140-3 Level 3 HSMs. Geo-distributed key storage with HA. Automated HSM cluster management.

controls.name: Key Rotation
controls.description: Implement automated cryptographic key rotation at defined intervals and upon compromise, ensuring encrypted data is re-encrypted with new keys without service disruption.

legal: PCI DSS 3.6.1.4 — Crypto-period limits
NIST SP 800-57 — Key rotation guidance
technical: Automated key rotation mechanisms
Re-encryption capability without downtime
Key version tracking and management
governance: Key rotation schedule defined per key type
Emergency rotation procedures for compromised keys

keyActivities.activity: Define rotation schedules by key type (1 year for data keys, 2 years for master keys)
Implement automated key rotation
Test emergency key rotation procedures
Verify re-encryption after key rotation

basic.description: Manual key rotation at annual intervals. Limited re-encryption capability.

mature.description: Automated key rotation for most key types. Key versioning with automatic re-encryption.

advanced.description: Continuous key rotation with envelope encryption. Zero-downtime key rotation across all services.

controls.name: Key Destruction
controls.description: Implement secure key destruction procedures ensuring retired or compromised keys are irreversibly destroyed and cannot be recovered, while maintaining audit trails.

legal: PCI DSS 3.6.1.4 — Key retirement/replacement
NIST SP 800-57 — Key destruction
technical: Cryptographic erasure of keys in HSM/key vault
Verification of key destruction completion
Audit logging of all key destruction events

keyActivities.activity: Define key destruction procedures per key type
Execute key destruction with dual control
Verify key destruction and log audit trail
Audit key destruction logs

basic.description: Manual key destruction with documented procedures. Audit logs maintained.

mature.description: Automated key destruction workflows. HSM-verified key zeroization. Dual-control enforcement.

advanced.description: Automated lifecycle-driven key destruction. Crypto-shredding for end-of-life data. Tamper-evident destruction logs.

controls.name: BYOK / HYOK Management
controls.description: Implement Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) or Hold Your Own Key (HYOK) strategies for cloud services, ensuring organizational control over encryption keys while leveraging cloud-native encryption.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.47 — Customer control of encryption keys in cloud
CSA CCM CEK-04 — Cloud key management
technical: BYOK key import to cloud provider KMS
HYOK with on-premises HSM for highly sensitive data
Key wrapping for BYOK transport
governance: Cloud provider key management capabilities assessed

keyActivities.activity: Assess BYOK vs. HYOK requirements per cloud workload
Implement BYOK key import for cloud KMS
Maintain on-premises HSM for HYOK scenarios
Audit cloud key usage and access patterns

basic.description: BYOK implemented for primary cloud provider. Basic key import procedures.

mature.description: BYOK across all cloud providers. HYOK for highly sensitive workloads. Automated key lifecycle management.

advanced.description: Multi-cloud BYOK with unified key management. HYOK with external key managers. Customer-controlled key hierarchy.

controls.name: Backup Encryption
controls.description: Ensure all backup data is encrypted both at rest and in transit using strong encryption algorithms, with backup encryption keys managed separately from production keys.

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Encrypted backups
BNM RMiT S10.46 — Encryption of backup data
technical: AES-256 encryption for all backup data
Backup encryption keys stored in separate key management system
Encrypted tape/media for offsite backups

keyActivities.activity: Enable encryption on all backup jobs
Store backup keys separately from backup media
Test encrypted backup restorability
Rotate backup encryption keys

basic.description: Backups encrypted with software-based encryption. Keys stored in documented location.

mature.description: Hardware-encrypted backups. Key management separated from backup infrastructure. Automated compliance verification.

advanced.description: Immutable encrypted backups with client-side encryption. Zero-knowledge backup architecture.

controls.name: Immutable Backups
controls.description: Implement immutable backup storage ensuring that backup data cannot be modified, encrypted by ransomware, or deleted before the defined retention period expires.

legal: Regulatory requirements for data retention integrity
technical: WORM (Write Once Read Many) storage for backups
Object lock or immutability policies on cloud backup storage
Multi-factor authentication for backup deletion
governance: Retention periods aligned with regulatory requirements

keyActivities.activity: Implement WORM storage for critical backups
Enable object lock on cloud backup storage
Require MFA for any backup deletion requests
Verify immutability controls quarterly

basic.description: Restricted delete permissions on backup storage. Basic retention enforcement.

mature.description: WORM storage deployed. Object lock enabled. MFA for backup deletion. Compliance monitoring.

advanced.description: Air-gapped immutable backups. Blockchain-verified backup integrity. Automated retention management.

controls.name: Air-Gapped Backup Copies
controls.description: Maintain air-gapped (offline) backup copies that are physically isolated from production networks, providing last-resort recovery capability against ransomware and insider threats.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.62 — Offline backup copies for critical systems
technical: Physically isolated backup media (tape, detached disk)
Secure transport and storage of offline media
Regular refresh of air-gapped copies
governance: Chain of custody for offline media

keyActivities.activity: Create air-gapped backup copies of critical data
Transport offline media to secure offsite storage
Test restore from air-gapped copies
Maintain chain of custody records

basic.description: Periodic tape backups stored offsite. Manual chain of custody tracking.

mature.description: Scheduled air-gapped copies with automated verification. Secure vault storage. Regular restore testing.

advanced.description: Multi-site air-gapped copies. Automated tape rotation with robotic libraries. Crypto-verified integrity on restore.

controls.name: Recovery Testing
controls.description: Conduct regular backup recovery testing to verify data integrity, measure recovery time objectives (RTO), and validate recovery point objectives (RPO) for all critical systems.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.63 — Regular recovery testing
PCI DSS 12.10.2 — Test restore procedures
technical: Automated recovery testing for critical databases
RTO/RPO measurement and tracking
Recovery test in isolated environment

keyActivities.activity: Conduct monthly recovery tests for critical systems
Measure and document RTO/RPO achievement
Conduct annual full disaster recovery exercise
Report recovery test results to management

basic.description: Quarterly recovery tests for critical systems. Manual RTO/RPO tracking.

mature.description: Monthly automated recovery testing. RTO/RPO dashboard. Annual DR exercise.

advanced.description: Continuous recovery verification. Chaos engineering for backup systems. Automated failover testing.

controls.name: Ransomware Resilience
controls.description: Implement ransomware-specific backup resilience controls including detection of backup corruption, isolated recovery environments, and rapid restore capabilities.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.64 — Ransomware preparedness
technical: Anomaly detection on backup data patterns
Clean room recovery environment
Rapid restore capability meeting RTO < 4 hours for critical systems

keyActivities.activity: Implement backup anomaly detection for ransomware indicators
Maintain clean room recovery environment
Conduct ransomware recovery tabletop exercise
Validate rapid restore capabilities against RTO targets

basic.description: Basic ransomware recovery procedures documented. Air-gapped backups available.

mature.description: Ransomware detection on backup streams. Clean room recovery tested. Regular tabletop exercises.

advanced.description: AI-driven ransomware detection in backups. Automated clean room provisioning. Sub-1-hour RTO for critical systems.

controls.name: Database Activity Monitoring
controls.description: Implement Database Activity Monitoring (DAM) solutions to capture, alert, and report on all database activities including privileged user actions, schema changes, and data access patterns.

legal: PCI DSS 10.2.1 — Log all access to cardholder data
BNM RMiT S10.50 — Database monitoring
technical: Agent-based or network-based DAM deployment
Real-time alerting on suspicious queries
Integration with SIEM for correlated analysis

keyActivities.activity: Deploy DAM on all databases with sensitive data
Configure alerts for privileged actions and bulk data access
Review DAM alerts and investigate anomalies
Generate compliance reports from DAM data

basic.description: Native database audit logging enabled. Manual log review.

mature.description: DAM deployed across all sensitive databases. Real-time alerting. SIEM integration.

advanced.description: ML-based anomaly detection on database access patterns. Behavioral profiling of database users.

controls.name: Database Access Controls
controls.description: Implement granular database access controls following the principle of least privilege, including role-based access, row-level security, and privileged access management for DBAs.

legal: PCI DSS 7.2.1 — Restrict access based on need-to-know
ISO 27001:2022 A.8.3 — Access restriction
technical: Role-based access control (RBAC) for all databases
Row-level security for multi-tenant databases
Privileged access management (PAM) for DBA accounts

keyActivities.activity: Implement RBAC for all database instances
Deploy PAM for privileged database accounts
Conduct quarterly database access reviews
Remove dormant database accounts

basic.description: Basic RBAC on production databases. Shared admin accounts still in use.

mature.description: Granular RBAC with row-level security. PAM for all DBA access. Regular access reviews.

advanced.description: Just-in-time database access. Attribute-based access control (ABAC). Automated access certification.

controls.name: Database Masking
controls.description: Implement dynamic data masking (DDM) at the database level to protect sensitive data from unauthorized viewing while allowing authorized operations on masked data.

legal: PCI DSS 3.3.1 — Mask PAN when displayed
BNM RMiT S10.52 — Data masking for non-production
technical: Dynamic masking rules per column and user role
Static masking for non-production environments
Masking that preserves referential integrity
governance: Masking rules aligned with classification policy

keyActivities.activity: Identify columns requiring masking
Implement dynamic masking rules
Mask all sensitive data in non-production environments
Audit masking bypass permissions

basic.description: Static masking for key sensitive fields. Manual non-production data sanitization.

mature.description: Dynamic masking integrated with access controls. Automated non-production masking during refresh.

advanced.description: Context-aware dynamic masking. Automated discovery and masking of new sensitive columns.

controls.name: Database Virtual Patching
controls.description: Implement virtual patching capabilities for databases to provide interim protection against known vulnerabilities when immediate database patching is not feasible.

legal: PCI DSS 6.3.3 — Install critical patches within one month
technical: Database firewall with virtual patching capability
SQL injection prevention rules
Regular virtual patch rule updates
governance: Patch gap tracking and remediation timeline

keyActivities.activity: Deploy database firewall with virtual patching
Apply virtual patches within 48 hours of critical CVE release
Track virtual patch coverage vs. actual patching
Test virtual patch effectiveness

basic.description: Database firewall deployed for critical databases. Manual virtual patching.

mature.description: Automated virtual patching for all databases. Virtual patch tracking dashboard.

advanced.description: Zero-day virtual patching via behavioral analysis. Automated patch gap reporting.

controls.name: Database Encryption (Column-Level)
controls.description: Implement column-level encryption for highly sensitive database fields (PAN, SSN, medical records) providing field-level protection independent of storage-level encryption.

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Render PAN unreadable
Healthcare regulations requiring field-level encryption
technical: Column-level encryption for designated sensitive fields
Application-transparent encryption where possible
Key management for column encryption keys
governance: Column encryption standards per data type

keyActivities.activity: Identify columns requiring column-level encryption
Implement column encryption with appropriate algorithms
Manage column encryption keys separately
Monitor performance impact and optimize

basic.description: Column encryption for PCI-scoped fields. Manual key management.

mature.description: Column encryption for all highly sensitive fields. HSM-backed key management. Minimal performance impact.

advanced.description: Always Encrypted with secure enclaves. Searchable encryption for encrypted columns.

controls.name: Static Data Masking
controls.description: Implement static data masking (SDM) to create sanitized copies of production data for use in development, testing, training, and analytics environments.

legal: BNM RMiT S10.52 — Non-production data masking
PCI DSS 6.5.1 — No production PAN in test
technical: SDM tool with consistent masking algorithms
Referential integrity preservation across related tables
Automated masking integrated with data refresh pipelines

keyActivities.activity: Deploy static masking tool for non-production data provisioning
Define masking rules preserving referential integrity
Automate masking in data refresh pipelines
Verify masking completeness before non-production release

basic.description: Manual data masking for key fields. Verification by sampling.

mature.description: Automated SDM in data refresh pipeline. Full referential integrity. Verification reports.

advanced.description: AI-driven masking rule discovery. Synthetic data generation. Fully automated DevOps integration.

controls.name: Dynamic Data Masking
controls.description: Implement dynamic data masking (DDM) to mask sensitive data in real-time during query results based on user roles and privileges, without altering stored data.

legal: PCI DSS 3.3.1 — Mask PAN when displayed
Need-to-know enforcement regulations
technical: DDM rules configured per column and role
Masking applied at query result level
No performance degradation beyond acceptable thresholds
governance: DDM rules documented per data element

keyActivities.activity: Identify data elements for dynamic masking
Configure DDM rules by role and data sensitivity
Test DDM effectiveness for each role
Monitor DDM performance impact

basic.description: Basic DDM for key sensitive fields (last 4 digits of PAN, etc.).

mature.description: Comprehensive DDM across all sensitive columns. Role-based masking policies.

advanced.description: Context-aware DDM with real-time policy evaluation. Integration with data catalog for automatic rule generation.

controls.name: Format-Preserving Tokenization
controls.description: Implement format-preserving tokenization (FPT) to replace sensitive data with tokens that maintain the original data format and length, enabling processing without schema changes.

legal: PCI DSS 3.5.1 — Tokenization as protection method
PCI TSP guidance for tokenization
technical: FPE/FPT implementation (FF1, FF3-1 algorithms)
Centralized token vault with high availability
Token-to-data mapping secured with encryption

keyActivities.activity: Deploy format-preserving tokenization platform
Tokenize PAN and other sensitive identifiers
Secure token vault with encryption and access controls
Test detokenization performance and availability

basic.description: Basic tokenization for PAN. Single-site token vault.

mature.description: Enterprise tokenization platform. Multi-data-type support. HA token vault.

advanced.description: Vaultless tokenization. Distributed FPT across multi-cloud. NIST-approved FPE algorithms.

controls.name: Test Data Management
controls.description: Implement test data management (TDM) controls ensuring non-production environments never contain real sensitive data, using synthetic data generation or masked copies.

legal: PCI DSS 6.5.1 — No production PAN in test/dev
BNM RMiT S10.52 — Test data controls
technical: Synthetic data generation capabilities
Masked production data for integration testing
Automated test data provisioning pipelines
governance: TDM policy prohibiting production data in non-production
Test data request and approval process

keyActivities.activity: Implement synthetic data generation for development
Prohibit production data in non-production environments
Scan non-production environments for production data
Automate test data provisioning with masking

basic.description: Manual data sanitization for test environments. Policy documented but enforcement is inconsistent.

mature.description: Automated test data provisioning with masking. Regular scans for production data in non-production.

advanced.description: AI-driven synthetic data generation. On-demand test data provisioning. Continuous compliance monitoring.

controls.name: De-identification & Anonymization
controls.description: Implement data de-identification and anonymization techniques for analytics, research, and data sharing scenarios to minimize re-identification risk.

legal: Privacy regulations requiring anonymization for secondary use
PDPA requirements for de-identification
technical: K-anonymity, l-diversity, or t-closeness algorithms
Re-identification risk assessment tools
Differential privacy for analytics outputs
governance: Re-identification risk assessment before data release

keyActivities.activity: Define de-identification standards per data type
Implement anonymization algorithms for data sharing
Conduct re-identification risk assessment
Monitor for re-identification attempts

basic.description: Basic pseudonymization for data sharing. Manual risk assessment.

mature.description: Formal de-identification framework. K-anonymity applied. Automated risk scoring.

advanced.description: Differential privacy for analytics. Privacy-preserving data publishing. Continuous re-identification risk monitoring.

controls.name: Media Sanitization
controls.description: Implement secure media sanitization procedures for all storage media (HDDs, SSDs, tapes, USB drives) prior to disposal, reuse, or transfer, following NIST SP 800-88 guidelines.

legal: NIST SP 800-88 Rev. 1 — Guidelines for Media Sanitization
PCI DSS 9.4.6 — Destroy media when no longer needed
technical: NIST 800-88 clear/purge/destroy methods
SSD-specific sanitization (crypto erase, block erase)
Degaussing for magnetic media
governance: Media sanitization procedures per media type
Certificate of destruction for all sanitized media

keyActivities.activity: Sanitize all storage media before disposal or reuse
Use appropriate method per media type (clear/purge/destroy)
Generate certificate of destruction for each media item
Audit media sanitization records

basic.description: Manual disk wiping tools. Paper-based destruction records.

mature.description: Certified sanitization tools. Automated certificate generation. Media tracking from asset register.

advanced.description: Automated sanitization workflows triggered by asset decommission. NIST 800-88 compliant with cryptographic verification.

controls.name: Crypto-Shredding
controls.description: Implement crypto-shredding (cryptographic erasure) as a data disposal method by destroying encryption keys, rendering encrypted data permanently unrecoverable.

legal: NIST SP 800-88 — Cryptographic erasure as purge method
Cloud-specific data disposal requirements
technical: Encryption key destruction verified and logged
All copies of encryption key destroyed (primary + backup)
Verification that encrypted data is unrecoverable

keyActivities.activity: Identify data sets eligible for crypto-shredding
Destroy all copies of encryption keys for target data
Verify key destruction across all key stores
Document crypto-shredding with audit trail

basic.description: Crypto-shredding available for specific use cases. Manual key destruction.

mature.description: Crypto-shredding standard practice for cloud data disposal. Automated key destruction workflows.

advanced.description: Crypto-shredding integrated with data lifecycle management. Automated verification across multi-cloud key stores.

controls.name: Certificate of Destruction
controls.description: Maintain formal certificates of destruction for all data disposal activities, providing auditable evidence of secure data destruction for compliance and legal purposes.

legal: Regulatory requirements for destruction documentation
Legal hold obligations before destruction
technical: Automated certificate generation linked to disposal workflow
Digital signatures on certificates
Certificate repository with retention
governance: Certificates retained for defined period (minimum 7 years)

Include serial numbers, methods, dates, and responsible parties
Retain certificates for minimum 7 years
Audit certificate completeness and accuracy

basic.description: Paper-based certificates of destruction. Manual tracking.

mature.description: Digital certificates with e-signatures. Linked to asset management system. Automated retention.

advanced.description: Blockchain-anchored certificates. Automated certificate generation from disposal workflow. Full chain of custody.

controls.name: Cloud Data Deletion
controls.description: Implement and verify secure data deletion in cloud environments, addressing challenges of multi-tenancy, storage replication, and backup copies across cloud regions.

technical: Cloud provider deletion API with verification

mature.description: Crypto-shredding for cloud data. Automated deletion verification. Provider SOC 2 review.

advanced.description: Multi-cloud deletion orchestration. Customer-verifiable deletion. Automated compliance reporting.

controls.description: Implement comprehensive end-of-life data management covering data retention enforcement, legal hold management, and systematic disposal of data that has exceeded its retention period.

Legal hold requirements

technical: Automated retention policy enforcement
Legal hold management system
Data lifecycle management tools

Implement automated retention enforcement
Manage legal holds preventing premature disposal
Dispose of data exceeding retention period
